method: Platform statements for non-wheelbase products referenced by the compatibility pages. Direct-drive wheelbases are covered by wheelbases.v1.json; this file exists only for the legacy geared/belt wheels people upgrade from. Same rules: manufacturer sources with retrieval dates, null-beats-guess.

ps.condition: PlayStation-licensed - Logitech's store page lists PS5, PS4, PS3, and PC. A platform mode switch selects PS4, PS3, or PC operation; PS5 use is in PS5 games that support the wheel.

xbox.condition: No Xbox support - the G920 is the Xbox counterpart

records.notes: Logitech's store page states PS5/PS4/PS3/PC for the G29 explicitly; the game-compatibility list's 'PlayStation 5 & 4' column corroborates. The mode-switch article predates PS5 and covers PS4/PS3/PC operation.

ps.condition: No PlayStation support - PlayStation appears nowhere on Logitech's G920 specifications page; the G29 and the G923 PlayStation version are the PlayStation counterparts

xbox.condition: Xbox Series X|S and Xbox One (Logitech specifications page)

records.notes: Spec page lists Windows 10/11 and Xbox Series X|S / Xbox One explicitly.

ps.condition: Only the PlayStation/PC version - Logitech's own download page titles it 'for PS5, PS4, and PC'

xbox.condition: Only the separately sold Xbox/PC version - Logitech's specification page titles it 'for Xbox One and PC'

records.notes: The console license is fixed by which version you buy; the two versions are separate SKUs.
